title: Aureline end-of-support and breaking-compatibility notice record
description: Boundary schema for the end_of_support_notice_record frozen in docs/release/end_of_support_and_migration_contract.md. The record carries one notice class (approaching_end_of_support, past_end_of_support, active_deprecation, breaking_compatibility_ahead), the exact cutoff (calendar date, release version, calendar window, or version window), the machine-readable affected scope (build, channel, extensions, automations, schemas, profiles, workspace or layout schemas, remote agents, helpers, CLI or SDK, marketplace, support-export format), at least one factual what_still_works projection, at least one typed risk_after_cutoff projection, exactly one safest_next_step, the required surface set the notice MUST render onto, and the cross-links into the channel-and-support-window badge, the compatibility-window report, and the migration-helper handoff packet so consuming surfaces never reconstruct the notice from prose. Generic fear copy ("end of support" with no factual what-still-works, no typed risk class, and no named safest next step) is non-conforming. Raw advisory bodies, raw policy bodies, raw release prose, raw URLs, raw signing material, and raw artifact bytes never appear; records carry refs, typed states, and bounded reviewable summaries only.

end_of_support_notice_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the end-of-support notice payload shape. Current value is 1.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable identifier safe for live surfaces, support bundles, public-proof packets, diagnostics exports, release packets, and migration exports. Raw URLs, raw hostnames, raw absolute paths, raw policy rule bodies, and raw secret material never appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence. Raw release prose, raw URLs, raw advisory bodies, raw incident bodies, raw policy bodies, and raw artifact bytes MUST NOT appear.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures; never part of the runtime record.

notice_class.description: Closed notice-class vocabulary. Each class binds different cutoff, evidence, and recovery rules in the schema's allOf block.

notice_severity_class.description: Closed severity vocabulary. Generic fear severities ("warning", "danger") are not admitted; severity is bound to a named factual posture.

cutoff_kind_class.description: Closed cutoff-kind vocabulary. Approaching, past, and active-deprecation classes MUST quote a concrete calendar or version cutoff; breaking-compatibility-ahead MAY quote a calendar window, a version window, or a not-yet-set posture if the change is still pending council review.

affected_scope_class.description: Closed affected-scope vocabulary. Every notice MUST bind at least one scope class so the affected surface set is never implicit.

risk_after_cutoff_class.description: Closed risk-after-cutoff vocabulary. Generic risk copy ("things may break") is non-conforming; every notice MUST bind at least one typed risk class.

what_still_works_class.description: Closed what-still-works vocabulary. The notice MUST bind at least one factual what-still-works projection so the banner cannot collapse to fear copy.

safest_next_step_class.description: Closed safest-next-step vocabulary. Every notice MUST bind exactly one typed safest_next_step. The banner copy resolves against this class; free-text "upgrade now" calls to action are non-conforming.

required_surface_class.description: Closed surface-class vocabulary. The four required floor surfaces are update_center_banner, about_panel_banner, compatibility_report_banner, and migration_or_import_workflow_banner; the remaining classes are admissible secondary surfaces.

cutoff_at.description: Exact calendar cutoff for exact_calendar_date kind. Null for exact_release_version, range_calendar_window, range_version_window, and not_yet_set_pending_council_review kinds.

cutoff_window_starts_at.description: Window start for range_calendar_window kind. Null otherwise.

cutoff_window_ends_at.description: Window end for range_calendar_window kind. Null otherwise.

cutoff_version_label.description: Reviewable version label for exact_release_version and range_version_window kinds (e.g. "2.0.0" or "2.x to 3.x"). Null otherwise.

cutoff_release_artifact_ref.description: Stable ref into /artifacts/release/channel_matrix.yaml or /docs/release/release_artifact_graph.md for the release artifact carrying the cutoff. Null when the cutoff is not yet bound to a published release.

cutoff_target.description: Cutoff target. Notices for approaching, past, and active-deprecation classes MUST resolve to a concrete calendar or version cutoff; breaking-compatibility-ahead MAY resolve to a window or a not-yet-set posture if the change is still pending council review.

build_identity_refs.description: Optional list of exact-build identity refs the notice narrows onto. Empty when the notice is channel-wide or category-wide.

channel_row_refs.description: Optional list of /artifacts/release/channel_matrix.yaml channel-row refs the notice applies to. Empty when the notice is build-wide or category-wide.

support_window_badge_refs.description: Optional list of /schemas/release/support_window_badge.schema.json badge ids the notice supersedes. Empty when no badge yet exists for the affected scope.

compatibility_row_refs.description: Optional list of /schemas/release/compatibility_row.schema.json row ids the notice narrows. Empty when no compatibility row yet exists for the affected scope.

compatibility_window_report_ref.description: Stable ref into a compatibility_window_report_record carrying the row-by-row breakdown. Required when the affected scope includes extensions, automations, command_or_action_schemas, profiles_and_settings, workspace_or_layout_schemas, remote_agents, or helpers_or_sidecars; null otherwise.

affected_scope_envelope.description: Scope envelope. The notice MUST bind at least one affected_scope_class; build, channel, badge, and row refs are admissible narrowing axes.

evidence_refs.description: Optional refs into a compatibility row, certified-archetype report, or release-evidence packet that backs the what-still-works claim. Empty for not_applicable_breaking_change_only.
Optional refs into a compatibility row, version-skew register, certified-archetype report, or supply-chain trust-framework matrix that backs the risk projection.

next_step_route_ref.description: Stable route ref into the surface that performs the safest next step (e.g. update center route, migration workflow route, compatibility report route). Null only for no_action_required_continue_inspecting and pause_until_named_release_lands_on_stable.

migration_helper_handoff_ref.description: Stable ref into a /schemas/release/compatibility_window_report.schema.json migration_helper_handoff block when the safest next step is run_migration_helper_handoff_with_required_backup. Null otherwise.

compatibility_window_report_ref.description: Stable ref into a compatibility_window_report_record. Required when the safest next step is migrate_extensions_via_compatibility_window_report, run_migration_helper_handoff_with_required_backup, or export_environment_summary_then_review_compatibility_window_report; null otherwise.

required_surface_set.description: Set of surfaces this notice MUST render onto. The four required floor surfaces (update_center_banner, about_panel_banner, compatibility_report_banner, migration_or_import_workflow_banner) are mandatory; tooling MAY add more, but MUST NOT drop one.

notice_id.description: Stable notice id. Quoted by update-center banners, About panels, compatibility reports, migration / import workflows, support exports, and release-notes cards.

headline_label.description: Bounded reviewable headline label rendered onto the banner. Free-text fear copy is non-conforming; the headline MUST resolve against the notice_class plus the safest_next_step_class.

what_still_works.description: At least one factual what-still-works projection. The schema's per-notice-class allOf block forbids the not_applicable_breaking_change_only class on approaching and past notices.

risk_after_cutoff.description: At least one typed risk projection. The schema's per-notice-class allOf block forbids the not_applicable_pre_release_only class on past_end_of_support notices.

allOf.description: approaching_end_of_support and past_end_of_support notices MUST resolve to an exact calendar date, an exact release version, a calendar window, or a version window; the not_yet_set_pending_council_review cutoff kind is forbidden because the cutoff is, by definition, already known. They MUST NOT use the not_applicable_breaking_change_only what-still-works class because the build is still installable.
past_end_of_support notices forbid the not_applicable_pre_release_only risk class because the affected build has reached general-availability cutoff and the risk is no longer pre-release-only.

breaking_compatibility_ahead notices MUST be inspectable and exportable BEFORE the platform change ships as stable or LTS truth. The safest next step MUST be export_environment_summary_then_review_compatibility_window_report or migrate_extensions_via_compatibility_window_report or run_migration_helper_handoff_with_required_backup or pause_until_named_release_lands_on_stable, and the affected scope MUST cite a non-null compatibility_window_report_ref.
active_deprecation notices MUST resolve to an exact calendar or version cutoff so the deprecation window is bounded; the not_yet_set kind is forbidden.
exact_calendar_date cutoffs MUST quote a non-null cutoff_at timestamp.
range_calendar_window cutoffs MUST quote both a non-null cutoff_window_starts_at and a non-null cutoff_window_ends_at.
exact_release_version and range_version_window cutoffs MUST quote a non-null cutoff_version_label so the version cutoff is reviewable.

When the affected scope includes extensions, automations_or_macros, command_or_action_schemas, profiles_and_settings, workspace_or_layout_schemas, remote_agents, or helpers_or_sidecars, the notice MUST cite a non-null compatibility_window_report_ref so consuming surfaces can pivot in O(1) into the row-by-row breakdown.

When the safest next step is migrate_extensions_via_compatibility_window_report, run_migration_helper_handoff_with_required_backup, or export_environment_summary_then_review_compatibility_window_report, the safest_next_step block MUST cite a non-null compatibility_window_report_ref.
When the safest next step is run_migration_helper_handoff_with_required_backup, the safest_next_step block MUST cite a non-null migration_helper_handoff_ref.
The required surface set MUST contain the four floor surfaces: update_center_banner, about_panel_banner, compatibility_report_banner, and migration_or_import_workflow_banner.
